---
title: "Lecture 3. Random variables and probability densities"
subtitle: "Statistical Data Analysis"
author: "Dmitry V. Naumov (JINR)"
format:
  revealjs:
    theme: black
    width: 1024
    height: 768
    slide-number: true
    preview-links: auto
    chalkboard:
      buttons: false
    logo: img/dvnlogo.png
    css: styles.css
    transition: fade
    center: true
---

# Lecture 3

## Random variables and probability densities

---

## Draft Outline

- TODO: discrete vs continuous random variables
- TODO: PDF, CDF, normalization
- TODO: transformations of variables

---

## Exercises

::: {.callout-warning .exbox appearance="simple"}
## Exercise

Show that the likelihood for a Poisson count $n$ with expectation $\mu$ is
$$
L(\mu)=\frac{\mu^n e^{-\mu}}{n!}.
$$
:::
